Home › Scholarships › Turkey and Canada
Turkey and Canada

Turkey and Canada's Joint Fellowship for Migrant and Refugee Researchers

Published 21 Jun 20266 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 21 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to Turkey and Canada's Joint Fellowship

The Turkey and Canada Joint Fellowship for Migrant and Refugee Researchers is a unique opportunity for postdoctoral researchers who are migrants or refugees to pursue their research interests in Turkey and Canada. This fellowship aims to support researchers who have been displaced due to conflict, persecution, or other circumstances and provide them with a platform to continue their academic pursuits. The fellowship is a result of a collaboration between the governments of Turkey and Canada, and it is designed to promote academic excellence and diversity.

Who Qualifies

To be eligible for the Turkey and Canada Joint Fellowship, applicants must meet specific visa requirements. The following visa categories are eligible to apply:

* Refugee visa

* Asylum visa

* Protection visa

* Humanitarian visa

* Temporary resident visa with a study or work permit

* Permanent resident visa with a study or work permit

Available Scholarships

The Turkey and Canada Joint Fellowship offers several scholarships to support migrant and refugee researchers. The following are five available scholarships:

Canada-Turkey Research Fellowship

The Canada-Turkey Research Fellowship provides a coverage amount of up to $50,000 per year for two years. Eligibility includes having a Ph.D. degree and being a migrant or refugee researcher. The deadline for application is February 1, 2027. For more information, please visit the official website at https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship.html.

Turkey-Canada Graduate Fellowship

The Turkey-Canada Graduate Fellowship provides a coverage amount of up to $30,000 per year for one year. Eligibility includes being enrolled in a graduate program and being a migrant or refugee researcher. The deadline for application is October 15, 2026. For more information, please visit the official website at https://www.turkiye.gov.tr/en.

Migrant and Refugee Research Fellowship

The Migrant and Refugee Research Fellowship provides a coverage amount of up to $40,000 per year for one year. Eligibility includes having a research proposal and being a migrant or refugee researcher. The deadline for application is May 1, 2027. For more information, please visit the official website at https://www.scholarships.gov.tr.

Postdoctoral Research Fellowship

The Postdoctoral Research Fellowship provides a coverage amount of up to $60,000 per year for two years. Eligibility includes having a Ph.D. degree and being a migrant or refugee researcher. The deadline for application is January 10, 2027. For more information, please visit the official website at https://www.cic.gc.ca.

International Research Fellowship

The International Research Fellowship provides a coverage amount of up to $70,000 per year for two years. Eligibility includes having a research proposal and being a migrant or refugee researcher. The deadline for application is March 15, 2027. For more information, please visit the official website at https://www.international.gc.ca.

Application Steps

To apply for the Turkey and Canada Joint Fellowship, follow these steps:

  • Check the eligibility criteria and ensure you meet the requirements.

  • Prepare your research proposal and gather all necessary documents.

  • Submit your application through the official website.

  • Upload all required documents, including your resume, transcripts, and letters of recommendation.

  • Pay the application fee, if applicable.

  • Wait for the review process to be completed.

  • Receive notification of the outcome of your application.

  • If selected, accept the offer and begin the visa application process.

Documents Checklist

The following documents are required to apply for the Turkey and Canada Joint Fellowship:

* Passport

* Visa

* Research proposal

* Resume

* Transcripts

* Letters of recommendation

* Proof of language proficiency

* Proof of financial support

Frequently Asked Questions

  • What is the Turkey and Canada Joint Fellowship?

The Turkey and Canada Joint Fellowship is a program that provides financial support to migrant and refugee researchers to pursue their research interests in Turkey and Canada. It is a unique opportunity for postdoctoral researchers who have been displaced due to conflict, persecution, or other circumstances. The fellowship is designed to promote academic excellence and diversity.

  • Who is eligible to apply for the fellowship?

To be eligible to apply for the fellowship, applicants must be migrant or refugee researchers who have a Ph.D. degree or are enrolled in a graduate program. They must also meet specific visa requirements, such as having a refugee visa or a temporary resident visa with a study or work permit.

  • What is the coverage amount of the fellowship?

The coverage amount of the fellowship varies depending on the specific scholarship. The Canada-Turkey Research Fellowship provides up to $50,000 per year for two years, while the Turkey-Canada Graduate Fellowship provides up to $30,000 per year for one year.

  • What is the deadline for application?

The deadline for application varies depending on the specific scholarship. The deadline for the Canada-Turkey Research Fellowship is February 1, 2027, while the deadline for the Turkey-Canada Graduate Fellowship is October 15, 2026.

  • How do I apply for the fellowship?

To apply for the fellowship, applicants must submit their application through the official website. They must upload all required documents, including their research proposal, resume, and transcripts.

  • What documents are required to apply for the fellowship?

The documents required to apply for the fellowship include a passport, visa, research proposal, resume, transcripts, letters of recommendation, proof of language proficiency, and proof of financial support.

  • Can I apply for multiple scholarships?

Yes, applicants can apply for multiple scholarships. However, they must meet the eligibility criteria for each scholarship and submit separate applications.

  • How do I contact the fellowship program?

For more information about the fellowship program, applicants can visit the official website at https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship.html or https://www.turkiye.gov.tr/en.

External Authority Links

For more information about the Turkey and Canada Joint Fellowship, please visit the official government websites at https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship.html and https://www.turkiye.gov.tr/en. These websites provide detailed information about the fellowship program, including eligibility criteria, application procedures, and deadlines.

Conclusion

The Turkey and Canada Joint Fellowship is a unique opportunity for migrant and refugee researchers to pursue their research interests in Turkey and Canada. To learn more about this fellowship and other opportunities for migrant and refugee researchers, please visit our blog at https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.
